title: Inula scaberrima
intro: Inula scaberrima, also known as the prickly elecampane, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family, characterized by its tall stem and yellow flowers. The plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and other infections. The bioactive constituents of Inula scaberrima include sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone, which have been identified for their therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Inula scaberrima is commonly prepared as a tincture or a decoction, and is often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Inula scaberrima belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is commonly known for its medicinal properties. It is a perennial herb, characterized by its yellow flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. The classification of Inula scaberrima is further categorized into the subfamily Carduoideae and tribe Cardueae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features.
